name: One-Pot Chicken Dinner (Nigerian)
recipeIngredient: 1 large chicken, cut into quarters or halves
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot or Dutch oven, place chicken, water, onion, celery, salt, and crushed pepper.
2. Cover and cook over moderately high heat until the mixture comes to a boil.
3. Reduce heat to moderately low and simmer for 30 minutes.
4. While the chicken is cooking, place tomatoes in a pan of boiling water for 1/2 minute. Remove skin and cut into eighths.
5. Add tomatoes, green pepper, lemon juice, tomato paste, and chili powder to the chicken pot.
6. Continue cooking for 10 minutes.
7. Add okra to the pot, cover, and cook another 15 minutes, stirring once or twice.
8. Serve hot in soup bowls with cooked rice on top.
When making cassaroles, I do not put the rice in with everything else if I am going to be freezing or storing it. White rice can be frozen in the cassarole, but brown rice gives a distinctly grainy flavor that some find disagreeable. So I will place the rice in a plastic ziploc bag and freeze it with the cassarole. Then, heat it separately before serving.
